title: Add Customer Photos Gallery

requirements.text: Add a backend data model to store customer photos, with each entry containing a unique photo ID, a name/label, the image data (stored as Blob or Base64-encoded Text), and an upload timestamp.

quotes: customer photos gallery iam earning customer photos save my app

acceptanceCriteria: A stable data structure exists in the Motoko actor to persist customer photo entries across canister upgrades.
Each entry has a unique ID, a label/name, image data, and a timestamp.
Data is stored in stable memory so photos survive upgrades.

requirements.text: Implement backend functions to upload a new customer photo (with name and image data), retrieve all saved customer photos, and delete a customer photo by ID.

quotes: customer photos gallery iam earning customer photos save my app

acceptanceCriteria: An `addCustomerPhoto` function accepts a name and Base64-encoded image data and stores it.
A `getCustomerPhotos` query function returns all saved customer photo entries.
A `deleteCustomerPhoto` function removes a photo entry by its ID.
Functions are callable from the frontend via the existing actor hook.

requirements.text: Create a Customer Photos Gallery page/section in the frontend where users can view all saved customer photos in a responsive grid layout, upload new photos (with a name label) using a file input, and delete existing photos with a confirmation prompt. The gallery should be accessible via a navigation tab or button in the app header alongside the existing commission dashboard.

quotes: customer photos gallery iam earning customer photos save my app

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Customer Photos' tab or button appears in the app header/navigation.
Clicking it shows a grid of all saved customer photos with their name labels.
Each photo card has a delete button that opens a confirmation dialog before removal.
An 'Upload Photo' button opens a form/dialog with a name input and a file picker accepting image files.
After upload, the gallery refreshes and the new photo appears in the grid.
The gallery uses the existing PhonePe-inspired indigo/magenta theme and card-based layout consistent with the rest of the app.
An empty state message is shown when no photos have been saved yet.

requirements.text: Add React Query hooks for the customer photos gallery: a query to fetch all customer photos and mutations to add and delete a customer photo, following the same pattern as the existing commission hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts.

quotes: customer photos gallery iam earning customer photos save my app

acceptanceCriteria: A `useCustomerPhotos` query hook fetches the list of photos from the backend.
An `useAddCustomerPhoto` mutation hook uploads a new photo and invalidates the photos query on success.
A `useDeleteCustomerPhoto` mutation hook deletes a photo by ID and invalidates the photos query on success.
Toast notifications are shown on upload/delete success and failure, consistent with existing mutation patterns.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
Image data must be stored within the canister (no external storage services).
The existing commission dashboard and all its features must remain fully functional.
nonGoals: External cloud storage (AWS S3, IPFS, etc.) for photos.
Sharing photos externally or social media integration.
User authentication changes beyond the existing anonymous/Internet Identity setup.
